items.title: Threat Brief: Exploitation of PAN-OS Captive Portal Zero-Day for Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution
items.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability in the User-ID Authentication Portal of PAN-OS software allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code with root privileges on PA-Series and VM-Series firewalls. Limited exploitation has been observed starting April 9, 2026, by a likely state-sponsored threat cluster. Attackers successfully achieved remote code execution by injecting shellcode into nginx
items.ai_summary: The exploitation of a zero-day buffer overflow vulnerability in the User-ID Authentication Portal of PAN-OS software allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code with root privileges on PA-Series and VM-Series firewalls. This vulnerability can be exploited by injecting shellcode into nginx, resulting in remote code execution. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it can allow attackers to gain control of the firewall and potentially access the entire network. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately apply the latest security patch to their PAN-OS software and ensure that their firewalls are running with the latest version of nginx. Additionally, defenders should monitor their network traffic for signs of exploitation and implement additional security measures, such as intrusion detection and prevention systems, to detect and prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1203| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Operation GriefLure: Dissecting an APT Campaign Targeting Vietnam's Military Telecom & Philippine Healthcare
items.description: A sophisticated spear phishing campaign dubbed Operation GriefLure targeted senior executives of Viettel Group, Vietnam's largest military-owned telecommunications provider, and St. Luke's Medical Center in the Philippines. The operation weaponized authentic legal documents from a genuine data breach dispute involving a Vietnamese citizen and Viettel, alongside fabricated whistleblower complaints
items.ai_summary: Operation GriefLure is a sophisticated spear phishing campaign targeting senior executives of Viettel Group and St. Luke's Medical Center, utilizing authentic legal documents and fabricated whistleblower complaints to deceive victims. The attack vector involves phishing emails, classified as T1566, which are designed to trick users into executing malicious code, categorized as T1204. If exploited, this campaign could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, potentially affecting national security and patient data. To remediate, defenders should implement email filters to detect and block phishing attempts, and educate users on how to identify and report suspicious emails. Additionally, organizations should conduct regular security audits to ensure the integrity of their systems and data.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1055| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: AI-Assisted Lure Factory Targets Developers & Gamers
items.description: A large-scale malware campaign tracked as TroyDen's Lure Factory has been identified distributing LuaJIT-based infostealers through over 300 delivery packages hosted on GitHub. The operation uses AI-generated lure names incorporating obscure biological taxonomy and medical terminology to target developers, gamers, Roblox players, and crypto users. The malware employs a two-component design with a
items.ai_summary: The TroyDen's Lure Factory campaign utilizes AI-generated lure names to target specific groups such as developers, gamers, and crypto users, distributing LuaJIT-based infostealers through over 300 delivery packages on GitHub. The malware employs a two-component design, with the first component responsible for initial access and the second for executing the infostealer. If exploited, this campaign can lead to significant data breaches, with potential victims including individuals and organizations in the gaming and development sectors. To remediate, defenders should implement strict access controls on GitHub repositories, monitor for suspicious LuaJIT executions, and educate users about the risks of AI-generated lures. Additionally, defenders can use network traffic analysis to detect and block communications with known command and control servers associated with this campaign.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Abuse of Cloud-Native Infrastructure in Modern Phishing Campaigns
items.description: An investigation has revealed a structural evolution in phishing operations where threat actors conduct entire campaigns through legitimate, enterprise-trusted cloud infrastructure rather than attacker-controlled systems. Adversaries weaponize platforms employees use daily, including cloud storage, productivity suites, and OAuth authentication endpoints. Attacks originate from legitimate Google or
items.ai_summary: The threat actors are leveraging cloud-native infrastructure to conduct phishing campaigns, utilizing legitimate platforms such as Google cloud storage and productivity suites to host malicious content. This approach allows them to bypass traditional security controls and increase the likelihood of successful phishing attempts. The real-world impact of this threat is significant, as it can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information and compromise of employee accounts. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement specific security controls, such as monitoring OAuth authentication endpoints for suspicious activity and enforcing multi-factor authentication for all users. Additionally, organizations should conduct regular security awareness training to educate employees on the risks of phishing and how to identify and report suspicious emails.

items.title: Agentic AI Uncovers New China-Linked Cluster OP-512
items.description: A newly identified China-linked espionage cluster designated OP-512 has been discovered targeting Internet Information Services (IIS) servers through advanced AI-driven detection. The operation involves deploying a sophisticated custom web shell framework consisting of three components: a file manager with command-and-control notification channel and two cryptographically authenticated command han
items.ai_summary: The OP-512 cluster, linked to China, has been identified as targeting Internet Information Services (IIS) servers using a custom web shell framework. This framework consists of three components, including a file manager and two cryptographically authenticated command handlers, which enable the threat actors to maintain command and control over compromised servers. The attack vector involves exploiting vulnerabilities in server software components, as indicated by the detection of TTP T1505, and leveraging application layer protocols, as seen with T1071. To remediate this threat, defenders should specifically focus on securing IIS servers by ensuring all components are up-to-date and implementing robust access controls, such as multi-factor authentication and strict firewall rules. Additionally, monitoring for unusual command-and-control communications and implementing a web application firewall (WAF) can help detect and prevent such attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1505| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Threat Brief: Active Exploitation of PAN-OS CVE-2026-0257
items.description: An unidentified threat actor is actively exploiting CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass vulnerability in PAN-OS GlobalProtect portal and gateway components. The flaw allows unauthorized attackers to circumvent security controls and initiate VPN connections. The vulnerability was added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on May 29, 2026. Exploitation activity has been detected tar
items.ai_summary: The active exploitation of PAN-OS CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass vulnerability in PAN-OS GlobalProtect portal and gateway components, allows unauthorized attackers to initiate VPN connections and circumvent security controls. This vulnerability affects PAN-OS versions prior to the patched version, which has not been specified, and can be exploited through the GlobalProtect portal and gateway components. Real-world impact includes unauthorized access to the network, potentially leading to lateral movement and data breaches. To remediate, defenders should immediately apply the patch for CVE-2026-0257, ensure that all GlobalProtect portal and gateway components are updated to the latest version, and monitor for suspicious VPN connection activity. Threat actor attribution has not been mentioned, but the vulnerability has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, indicating active exploitation in the wild.

items.title: Seeking Counsel: Ongoing Targeted Campaign Against US Law Firms
items.description: From January through May 2026, a financially motivated data theft extortion campaign executed by threat cluster UNC3753 targeted dozens of organizations across professional, legal, and financial services in the United States. The threat actors leverage voice phishing and social engineering techniques, posing as IT support to convince targets to host screen-sharing sessions and download remote moni
items.ai_summary: The UNC3753 threat cluster has been conducting a targeted campaign against US law firms from January to May 2026, utilizing voice phishing and social engineering tactics to trick victims into hosting screen-sharing sessions and downloading remote monitoring tools. The attackers pose as IT support personnel to gain the trust of their targets, leveraging T1598.004 spearphishing voice and T1566 phishing techniques to initiate the attack. Once access is gained, the threat actors use T1204 user execution to execute malicious code, followed by T1041 exfiltration over a command and control channel to steal sensitive data. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement multi-factor authentication, conduct regular security awareness training, and monitor for unusual network activity, specifically focusing on inbound and outbound traffic on non-standard ports. Additionally, law firms should verify the identity of anyone claiming to be IT support before allowing remote access, and consider implementing a least privilege approach to limit the damage in case of a breach.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1598.004| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: VerdantBamboo: Just Another BRICKSTORM in the Firewall
items.description: Chinese threat actor VerdantBamboo compromised a victim organization and its Managed Services Provider over an 18-month period, deploying malware on network edge devices lacking EDR coverage. The initial breach involved an Egnyte Storage Sync system, where attackers exploited a sudo misconfiguration for privilege escalation and installed BRICKSTORM backdoor and AGENTPSD fallback implant. Investiga
items.ai_summary: The VerdantBamboo threat actor exploited a sudo misconfiguration on an Egnyte Storage Sync system to gain initial access, leveraging T1068 for privilege escalation. This allowed them to install the BRICKSTORM backdoor and AGENTPSD fallback implant on network edge devices lacking Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) coverage. The real-world impact includes compromised Managed Services Providers and victim organizations, with attackers potentially gaining access to sensitive data and systems. To remediate, defenders should ensure proper sudo configuration, implement EDR on all network devices, and monitor for suspicious activity related to T1649 and T1219. Specifically, defenders should review their Egnyte Storage Sync systems for any signs of unauthorized access or malware installation, and update their systems to the latest version with secure configuration settings.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1068| B[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    B -->|T1649| C[Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Remote Access Software]:::tactic
    D -->|T1068| E[Exploitation for Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Miasma Worm Campaign Spreads with New PyPI Wave
items.description: A coordinated PyPI compromise campaign involving 37 malicious wheel artifacts across 19 packages was detected, utilizing Python startup hooks to execute credential-stealing payloads. The attack leverages .pth files for automatic execution during Python interpreter startup, downloads the Bun JavaScript runtime, and runs obfuscated JavaScript payloads. The malware targets high-value developer and CI
items.ai_summary: The Miasma Worm Campaign is a coordinated PyPI compromise campaign that involves 37 malicious wheel artifacts across 19 packages, utilizing Python startup hooks to execute credential-stealing payloads. The attack leverages .pth files for automatic execution during Python interpreter startup, downloads the Bun JavaScript runtime, and runs obfuscated JavaScript payloads. This campaign targets high-value developer and CI environments, potentially leading to significant data breaches and unauthorized access. To remediate, developers should inspect their Python environments for suspicious .pth files and remove any unknown or untrusted packages, ensuring they are using the latest versions of their dependencies. Additionally, implementing strict access controls and monitoring for unusual JavaScript runtime executions can help mitigate the impact of this campaign.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.007| B[Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    C -->|T1550| D[Credential Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Technical Advisory: Breach of Instructure Canvas LMS
items.description: In early May 2026, Instructure confirmed a breach affecting its Canvas learning platform after detecting unauthorized activity on May 1. ShinyHunters exploited the Free-For-Teacher account program, compromising the Canvas platform directly and exposing names, email addresses, student IDs, and private messages. The exposure window ran from April 30 to May 7, 2026. ShinyHunters claims 3.6 TB of data
items.ai_summary: The breach of Instructure Canvas LMS, a popular learning management system, occurred between April 30 and May 7, 2026, when threat actors exploited the Free-For-Teacher account program. This vulnerability allowed ShinyHunters to compromise the Canvas platform directly, resulting in the exposure of sensitive information including names, email addresses, student IDs, and private messages, with the attackers claiming to have obtained 3.6 TB of data. The real-world impact of this breach is significant, as it affects educational institutions and their students, potentially leading to identity theft, phishing attacks, and other malicious activities. To remediate this issue, affected institutions should immediately review and secure their Canvas configurations, ensuring that all accounts, especially those created under the Free-For-Teacher program, are properly vetted and monitored. Furthermore, institutions should notify and educate their users about the breach, advising them to be cautious of potential phishing attempts and to monitor their personal information for signs of unauthorized access.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1615| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    D -->|T1083| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-10115: A vulnerability was identified in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This affects an unknown p...
items.description: A vulnerability was identified in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This affects an unknown part in the library lib/sbi/nnrf-handler.c of the component Shared NF-profile Parser. Such manipulation leads to denial of service. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. It is advisable to implement a patch to correct this issue.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10115 is a vulnerability in Open5GS up to version 2.7.7, specifically affecting the lib/sbi/nnrf-handler.c component of the Shared NF-profile Parser. This vulnerability can be exploited remotely, leading to a denial of service. The CVSS score of 4.3 indicates a moderate severity level. To remediate this issue, defenders should apply the patch to update Open5GS to version 2.7.8 or later, and consider implementing network traffic filtering to limit the attack surface. Additionally, monitoring network traffic for signs of denial of service attacks, such as unusual packet flooding, can help defenders detect and respond to potential exploits.

items.title: CVE-2026-10114: A vulnerability was determined in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected by this issue is...
items.description: A vulnerability was determined in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected by this issue is the function handle_scp_info in the library lib/sbi/nnrf-handler.c of the component Shared NF-profile Parser. This manipulation causes out-of-bounds write. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. To fix this issue, it is recommended to deploy a patch.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10114 is a vulnerability in Open5GS up to version 2.7.7, specifically affecting the handle_scp_info function in the lib/sbi/nnrf-handler.c library of the Shared NF-profile Parser component. This issue allows for an out-of-bounds write, which can be initiated remotely, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution or data corruption. The real-world impact of this vulnerability could result in unauthorized access to sensitive network functions, compromising the security and integrity of the affected systems. To remediate this issue, defenders should deploy the recommended patch, ensuring they update to a version later than 2.7.7. Additionally, network administrators should monitor their systems for any suspicious activity and consider implementing additional security measures, such as intrusion detection and prevention systems, to detect and prevent potential exploitation.

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-9757: The GEO my WP plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the 'swlat...
items.description: The GEO my WP plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the 'swlatlng' and 'nelatlng' parameters in all versions up to, and including, 4.5.5 The parameters are read from $_SERVER['QUERY_STRING'] via parse_str() (bypassing WordPress's wp_magic_quotes protection, which only covers $_POST/$_GET/$_COOKIE/$_REQUEST), then each is split on ',' via explode() and the resulting fragments are
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9757 is a SQL Injection vulnerability in the GEO my WP plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to and including 4.5.5. The vulnerability is exploited via the 'swlatlng' and 'nelatlng' parameters, which are read from the query string and then split, allowing an attacker to inject malicious SQL code. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to extract or modify sensitive data from the database, potentially affecting all users of the plugin. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update the GEO my WP plugin to version 4.5.6 or later, and ensure that all input parameters are properly sanitized and validated. Additionally, defenders should monitor their web application logs for suspicious activity and consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent SQL Injection attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-7465: The Spectra Gutenberg Blocks – Website Builder for the Block Editor plugin for W...
items.description: The Spectra Gutenberg Blocks – Website Builder for the Block Editor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Remote Code Execution in all versions up to, and including, 2.19.25. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to execute code on the server. Exploitation requires a two-block payload embedded in post content: the first block registers a fake
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-7465 is a critical Remote Code Execution (RCE) vulnerability in the Spectra Gutenberg Blocks – Website Builder for the Block Editor plugin for WordPress, affecting all versions up to and including 2.19.25. An authenticated attacker with Contributor-level access and above can exploit this vulnerability by embedding a two-block payload in post content, allowing them to execute code on the server. This vulnerability can be exploited using the T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) tactic, and the T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) tactic is also involved in the exploitation process. To remediate this vulnerability, WordPress administrators should update the plugin to version 2.19.26 or later, and ensure that all users with Contributor-level access and above have strong, unique passwords. Additionally, it is recommended to implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to prevent exploitation attempts.

items.title: CVE-2026-7459: The Simple History – Track, Log, and Audit WordPress Changes plugin for WordPres...
items.description: The Simple History – Track, Log, and Audit WordPress Changes plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authenticated (Subscriber+) account takeover in all versions up to, and including, 5.26.0 via the event reaction endpoints (react_to_event() / unreact_to_event()). The endpoints register get_items_permissions_check() as their permission_callback, which only verifies the requester is logged in and doe
items.ai_summary: The Simple History – Track, Log, and Audit WordPress Changes plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authenticated account takeover in all versions up to and including 5.26.0. The vulnerability occurs due to the event reaction endpoints (react_to_event() / unreact_to_event()) registering get_items_permissions_check() as their permission_callback, which only verifies the requester is logged in. This allows attackers to exploit the vulnerability by sending a crafted request to the event reaction endpoints, resulting in account takeover. To remediate this issue, WordPress administrators should update the plugin to version 5.27.0 or later, and ensure that the 'Subscriber' role has restricted access to the event reaction endpoints. Additionally, it is recommended to implement additional security measures such as logging and auditing to detect and prevent similar attacks in the future.

items.description: The financially motivated group is combining vishing, IT impersonation, and in-person office intrusions to steal data and extort victims.

items.title: New Shai-Hulud attack trojanizes 19 science-focused PyPI packages
items.description: Hackers compromised 19 packages on the PyPI, collectively downloaded hundreds of thousands of times, in a new Shai-Hulud supply-chain attack that delivered malware designed to steal developer secrets. [...]

items.title: Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited Since Early May
items.description: A newly discovered, critical zero-day vulnerability is under attack; a Qilin ransomware affiliate has been blamed for at least one incident.

items.title: UK gives big tech 3 months to create device controls to block nude images of kids
items.description: The companies “must activate built-in features or implement technical solutions on smartphones and tablets to detect and block nude images for children,” according to a press release from the Home Office. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced the measure in a speech at London Tech Week Monday.

items.title: One-Character Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Local Root Access, Exploits Now Public
items.description: Security researchers have published a detailed, working exploit for a Linux kernel use-after-free that lets an unprivileged local user escalate to root and break out of a container. The flaw, CVE-2026-23111, sits in the kernel's nf_tables packet-filtering code and was patched upstream on February 5, 2026. Exodus Intelligence released its full technical walkthrough on June 8, and it is not even

items.title: Iran Signed a Ceasefire — Its Hackers Didn't
items.description: An extension of the Geneva Conventions could impose restrictions on cyberwarfare under ceasefire conditions and close a major loophole in international conflict.

items.title: WhatsApp says it disrupted new NSO spyware phishing attacks
items.description: WhatsApp has detected and stopped spear-phishing campaigns allegedly conducted by the NSO Group after investigating user reports of social engineering attacks. [...]

items.title: A Security Raises $37 Million for Autonomous Offensive Security Platform
items.description: The company founded by Yossi Torati, Omer Gull, and Yuval Itzchakov has emerged from stealth mode. The post A Security Raises $37 Million for Autonomous Offensive Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Armenia’s pro-Europe party wins election despite Russia-linked disinformation
items.description: Pashinyan's Civil Contract party won nearly 50% of Sunday's vote, defeating the pro-Russian Strong Armenia party led by Russian-Armenian billionaire Samvel Karapetyan, which received around 23% of the vote.

items.title: Meta Blocks NSO Group's New WhatsApp Phishing Attack, Files Contempt Order
items.description: Meta on Monday said it detected and blocked spear-phishing attempts linked to Israeli spyware vendor NSO Group. In addition, the tech giant said it's filing a federal court contempt order against the company for violating a permanent injunction that barred it from targeting WhatsApp and its users. "They tried to trick people into clicking on malicious links to drive them to external websites

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-06-07, (Mon, Jun 8th)
items.description: This diary continues the Internet Storm Center&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s tracking of the TeamPCP supply chain campaign, first documented in the SANS white paper When the Security Scanner Became the Weapon and most recently in the handler diary Activity Through 2026-05-24. Since that update, the story moved into two new places: the United States government, which formally caught up to the campaign, and the wider population of attackers now wielding the Mini Shai-Hulud framework that TeamPCP open-sourced last month.

items.title: WhatsApp says NSO targeted users with spearfishing attacks in violation of court order
items.description: WhatsApp said it is filing a federal court contempt order against NSO for violating a permanent injunction that bars it from mounting attacks against its users.

items.title: Gogs patches critical zero-day enabling remote code execution
items.description: Gogs has patched a critical security zero-day flaw that can allow attackers to compromise Internet-facing instances and access any repositories (including private ones). [...]

items.title: 'Hades' Campaign Against PyPI Puts New Spin on Shai-Hulud
items.description: The latest attacks, which hit 37 PyPI wheels and 19 code packages, show a continued evolution of the persistent software supply chain threat.

items.title: Critical UniFi OS bug lets hackers gain root without authentication
items.description: Attackers can chain three already fixed vulnerabilities in the Ubiquiti UniFi OS server to execute remote code with root privileges and without authentication. [...]

items.title: Everybody Is Vibe Coding But Nobody Told the Security Team
items.description: AI-driven development is not something organizations can or should block. But it must be governed. The post Everybody Is Vibe Coding But Nobody Told the Security Team appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited to Bypass Passwords in IKEv1 Setups
items.description: Check Point has warned of active exploitation of a critical vulnerability impacting Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments that are configured to use the deprecated IKEv1 key exchange protocol. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50751 (CVSS score: 9.3), is a case of a logic flow weakness in certificate validation that allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to bypass user

items.title: Russia upgrades rules for its digital spy system to better track citizens online
items.description: New regulations published by Russia's Ministry of Digital Development at the end of May updated the technical standards governing SORM, formally known as the System for Operative Investigative Activities.

items.title: Reducing security operations complexity with Wazuh Cloud
items.description: Security teams are increasingly overwhelmed by alert fatigue, infrastructure maintenance, and complex hybrid environments. This article explores how Wazuh Cloud helps simplify SIEM/XDR operations through managed infrastructure, automated scaling, and AI-driven security analysis. [...]

items.title: WhatsApp Catches Spyware Firm NSO Defying No-Hacking Court Order
items.description: The Meta-owned communications app is filing a federal court contempt order against NSO. The post WhatsApp Catches Spyware Firm NSO Defying No-Hacking Court Order appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AI Phishing Is Crushing SOCs with Alert Volume: How to Reduce Tier 1 Overload
items.description: Phishing has always been a numbers game. AI has turned it into a volume machine. Attackers can now create convincing emails, fake login pages, and tailored lures in minutes. Every polished message adds another case for Tier 1 to review, another link to inspect, and another alert that cannot be dismissed at a glance. As the queue grows, a credential theft attempt or malware delivery can easily

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Instagram Account Hacks, Android Zero-Day, GitHub Worm and More
items.description: Monday again. The weekend was meant to be quiet. It wasn't. Last week had poisoned packages, a broken AI helper, and a worm tearing through repos. The ugly part: basic tricks still worked. A chatbot got fooled. A bot token got leaked inside the malware. The same old mistakes showed up again. And while everyone chased the loud stuff, quieter attackers sat in inboxes for months, reading mail and

items.title: Check Point links VPN zero-day attacks to Qilin ransomware gang
items.description: Israeli cybersecurity company Check Point has released security updates to patch a critical flaw affecting Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments, which was exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

items.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 26 Deals Announced in May 2026
items.description: Significant cybersecurity M&A deals announced by Akamai, Check Point, Cisco, Cyera, Dragos, WatchGuard and Zscaler. The post Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 26 Deals Announced in May 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Everest Forms Vulnerability Exploited to Hack WordPress Sites
items.description: The flaw allows attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely and has been exploited in the wild for two months. The post Everest Forms Vulnerability Exploited to Hack WordPress Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: The Hardest Fork
items.description: Mythos is real. I know a big chunk of the industry thinks it's a marketing stunt, and I get why. I get it. But I've seen the findings, and they're bad. These aren't "whoops, this line right here is wrong, and that's RCE." They're novel combinations of a few dozen issues out of thousands of things every SAST scanner already finds, chained together into something much worse. It's real creativity,

items.title: 174,000 Impacted by Lansing Community College Data Breach
items.description: Hackers accessed personal information stored on certain Lansing Community College systems in February 2025. The post 174,000 Impacted by Lansing Community College Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Oxford University discloses data breach after careers platform hack
items.description: The University of Oxford disclosed a new data breach last week after being informed by its third-party provider, Group GTI, that its CareerConnect career services platform had been compromised. [...]

items.title: Silent Ransom Group Uses DNS Fast Flux in Attacks
items.description: Focusing on hacking law firms in the US, the ransomware group relies on fast flux to hide its C&C infrastructure. The post Silent Ransom Group Uses DNS Fast Flux in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: VerdantBamboo Deploys BSD Variant of BRICKSTORM on Linux Appliances
items.description: A China-nexus cyber espionage group has been observed deploying a BSD variant of a known backdoor called BRICKSTORM, as well as two other malware families codenamed PLENET (aka GRIMBOLT) and AGENTPSD to target Linux systems. The activity has been attributed by Volexity to a threat cluster it tracks as VerdantBamboo, which it said overlaps with hacking groups known as Clay Typhoon (Microsoft),

items.title: OpenAI Rolling Out ChatGPT Account Security Controls
items.description: The Active Sessions and Lockdown Mode features are being made more broadly available by the AI giant. The post OpenAI Rolling Out ChatGPT Account Security Controls appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Anthropic Urges Industry Coordination to Allow for a ‘Pause’ in AI Development if Risks Grow
items.description: The proposed coordination would let advanced AI labs verify that global rivals have actually stopped or slowed their work. The post Anthropic Urges Industry Coordination to Allow for a ‘Pause’ in AI Development if Risks Grow appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: UNC3753 Used Vishing and Physical Intrusions in U.S. Data Theft Extortion Campaign
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a financially motivated data theft extortion campaign that has targeted dozens of organizations across professional, legal, and financial services in the U.S. between January and May 2026. The activity has been attributed by Google Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) to a threat actor dubbed UNC3753, which is also known as

items.title: VS Code Adds 2-Hour Extension Auto-Update Delay to Limit Supply Chain Attacks
items.description: Microsoft has announced that Visual Studio Code (VS Code) will apply a two-hour delay before extensions for the integrated development environment (IDE) are updated automatically to a newer version in an attempt to tackle software supply chain threats. "When automatic updates are enabled, new versions are auto-updated two hours after they are published, adding an extra layer of protection

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 8th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9962, (Mon, Jun 8th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
items.description: Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.

items.title: Adaptive, Agentic AI Worms Loom as Next Enterprise Threat
items.description: AI worms, or "viruses with wings and brains," adapt to new environments, seek out vulnerabilities, and will likely strike within a year, researchers say.

items.title: EU unveils tech sovereignty package to cut reliance on US, Chinese suppliers
items.description: The package bundles two draft laws — a Chips Act 2.0 and a Cloud and AI Development Act (CADA) — alongside an Open Source Strategy and a roadmap for digitalizing the energy system.

items.title: Trump AI Order Seeks Voluntary Frontier Model Testing
items.description: The White House's executive order establishes voluntary framework for early government access to frontier models while investing in federal security.

items.title: The Evil MSI Background is Back!, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.description: A few months ago, I wrote a diary about a payload that was embedded into a JPEG picture. It was a MSI-branded background[1]. Yesterday, I spotted another one! It seems that the technic is getting more and more popular. This time, it started with a mail containing a WeTransfer link.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, June 5th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9960, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Rust-Written IronWorm Hits NPM Supply Chain
items.description: Like Shai-Hulud, the campaign targets developers to steal credentials and reuses them to propagate across the software supply channel.

items.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
items.description: One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.

items.title: 4 Critical Threats Where Attackers Have the Advantage
items.description: Gartner analysts issued a call to action to bolster defenses against several emerging critical threats, such as deepfakes and prompt injections.

items.title: Microsoft's Coreutils for Windows, (Thu, Jun 4th)
items.description: I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;ve been using the GnuWin32 CoreUtils for Windows for many years now (it gives you many *nix core commands on Windows).

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, June 4th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9958, (Thu, Jun 4th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Continuing Scans for swagger.json, (Wed, Jun 3rd)
items.description: Enterprise applications often still use complex standards like SOAP for web services. The big advantage of SOAP is its tight and extensive standards, which enable interoperability across an enterprise governed by web services. The disadvantage of SOAP: First, while it is de facto usually used over HTTP, it does not leverage HTTP, leading to unnecessary complexity. Secondly, kids don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t RTFM, and developers these days tend not to appreciate the art of careful system design; they rather throw code at an IDE to see what sticks, if they don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t vibe code it anyway.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, June 3rd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9956, (Wed, Jun 3rd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
New Wave Of Phishing Emails with SVG Files, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: For a few days, my SANS ISC mailbox is flooded with emails that delivers SVG files. An SVG ("Scalable Vector Graphic") is a web-friendly vector file format used for graphics and icons. No URL in the body, just â€œan imageâ€, that&#x27s the perfect way to deliver some malicious content. This isn&#x27t the first time that we see this technique used by threat actors[1].

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
